local_names.romanized_local: Jīròu tōngxīnfěn tāng (Chinese); Sup Makaroni Ayam (Malay); Cikkan Makroṇi Sūp (Tamil); Cikan Maikaronī Sūp (Hindi)

region_of_origin: Western / International; widely consumed in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'chicken soup' class (FOODON_00002572); no specific 'chicken macaroni soup' class exists. Closest defensible parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pasta dishes — soup-based pasta dish with chicken
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers pasta-based dishes; no exact code for chicken macaroni soup. Closest defensible parent code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chicken macaroni soup as a food substance.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of chicken macaroni soup is approximately 350 g, comprising broth, boiled macaroni, and pieces of cooked chicken.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken macaroni soup — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for Chicken macaroni soup (cr_id F05060029). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Macaroni, boiled — GI value 47 (analogous estimate for soup-diluted preparation)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for chicken macaroni soup. GI of 47 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled macaroni/pasta (GI ~47, low category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 47 × 22.4 g (6.4 g per 100 g × 350 g serving) / 100 = 10.5. The soup preparation dilutes carbohydrate density further, so GI 47 is a conservative upper-bound estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 354 mg per 100 g; a 350 g bowl delivers approximately 1,239 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a bowl, request reduced-sodium or homemade broth, avoid adding soy sauce or seasoning at the table, and prioritise the solid ingredients over the soup base to reduce sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 6.4 g per 100 g with low GI (~47) and high water content from broth results in a low glycaemic load per serving (GL ~10.5); protein 3.2 g per 100 g further moderates postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chicken macaroni soup is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in standard portions; choosing wholemeal or high-fibre pasta variants and monitoring total carbohydrate intake across the full meal is advised to maintain glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium content at 354 mg per 100 g is the primary cardiovascular concern; fat is low at 0.3 g per 100 g and saturated fat is negligible at 0.1 g per 100 g, which is favourable, but the high sodium load from chicken broth poses a risk for blood pressure management in CVD patients.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request low-sodium broth, limit soup volume to reduce cumulative sodium intake, and pair the dish with potassium-rich vegetables to partially offset the sodium load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined macaroni provides negligible anti-inflammatory nutrients; dietary fibre is very low at 0.2 g per 100 g and no omega-3 fatty acids or significant polyphenols are present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute refined macaroni with wholemeal pasta, add anti-inflammatory ingredients such as ginger or turmeric to the broth, and request reduced-sodium preparation to avoid exacerbating tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken macaroni soup is a light, broth-based soup dish consisting of boiled elbow macaroni and tender pieces of cooked chicken served in a clear or lightly seasoned chicken broth. It is a common comfort food and working-lunch option in Singapore, widely available at hawker centres, food courts, and school canteens. The dish has a mild, savoury flavour with a soft texture from the macaroni and moist chicken. Nutritionally, it is low in calories at 41 kcal per 100 g, low in fat at 0.3 g per 100 g, and provides moderate protein at 3.2 g per 100 g. The primary nutritional concern is sodium at 354 mg per 100 g, largely contributed by the chicken broth and any added seasoning; a standard 350 g bowl delivers approximately 1,239 mg sodium. Carbohydrate content is moderate at 6.4 g per 100 g with a low glycaemic index (~47 for boiled macaroni), making it relatively suitable for glycaemic management when consumed in controlled portions. Dietary fibre is low at 0.2 g per 100 g. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore food establishments and is suitable as a soft food for individuals with chewing difficulties. It is classified under grains and staples, noodle and pasta-based dishes, and is considered minimally processed. Clinically, it is most suitable for individuals managing weight or blood glucose, but should be consumed cautiously by those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium content.

confidence_reason: Core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo Food Composition Database (cr_id F05060029) at confidence 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore dishes. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest analogous food (boiled macaroni, GI 47) and is clearly documented as an analogous estimate.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific chicken macaroni soup preparation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry (F05060029) as of 2026-04-16. sugar_g estimated at 0.5 g per 100 g based on typical chicken broth and macaroni composition; saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.1 g per 100 g consistent with low total fat of 0.3 g per 100 g from lean chicken and plain macaroni. GI of 47 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (boiled macaroni); no direct GI measurement for chicken macaroni soup exists in the approved GI database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as 47 × 22.4 g / 100 = 10.5. This is a Western-origin dish widely consumed in Singapore and is subject to the multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes due to its widespread consumption and availability in Singaporean food environments.
